message:
feat(console): add ConsoleCaps detection + banner + help hint (br-1m6a.23)

Add ConsoleCaps struct computed from ftui TerminalCapabilities at
startup: true_color, osc8, mouse, sync, kitty, focus, in_mux. Emit
themed "Console Capabilities" banner section with check/cross markers
and mux warning. Grep-friendly one-liner (ConsoleCaps: tc=1 osc8=0...)
for E2E tests. Wire help_hint into config summary palette action.
8 unit tests for format stability, ASCII-only, banner content, mux
warning, help hint, field mapping.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -61,7 +61,7 @@
 {"id":"br-1m6a.2","title":"Tool call start/end logging panels with syntax highlighting","description":"Upgrade per-tool-call console logging to be FrankenTUI-native and parity-aligned with the legacy Python `rich_logger.py` tool panels.

CURRENT STATE
- Tool-call start/end panels already exist in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/console.rs`:
  - `render_tool_call_start()`
  - `render_tool_call_end()`
- They are currently hand-built with raw ANSI escape codes and simplistic JSON coloring.
- `dispatch_inner("tools/call")` in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs` emits these panels, but does not yet consistently respect the intended config gates.

GATING (ALL MUST PASS)
- `config.log_rich_enabled == true` (default true)
- `stdout.is_terminal() == true`
- `config.tools_log_enabled == true` (env `TOOLS_LOG_ENABLED`, default true)
- `config.log_tool_calls_enabled == true` (env `LOG_TOOL_CALLS_ENABLED`, default should become true via `br-1m6a.16`)

START PANEL (tool-call-start)
- Title: TOOL name + timestamp + project/agent hints.
- "Input Parameters" section:
  - Pretty JSON (2-space indent)
  - Apply masking first using `br-1m6a.18`.
  - Syntax highlight JSON using `ftui-extras` `syntax` (preferred):
    - `SyntaxHighlighter::highlight(..., "json")`
- Route output:
  - If StartupDashboard is active: write to scrollback via `dashboard_write_log()`.
  - Else: `ftui_runtime::ftui_println!()`.

END PANEL (tool-call-end)
- Rule separator color-coded by status.
- Summary line/table:
  - Tool, Project, Agent, Duration, Status
  - Query summary: total queries + total query time (from per-call tracker when instrumentation enabled)
- Duration tier styling:
  - <50ms: bright green (fast)
  - <100ms: green
  - <500ms: yellow
  - <1000ms: bright yellow
  - >=1000ms: red
- Result preview:
  - JSON pretty + syntax highlight (success)
  - Structured error object (failure)
  - Truncate BOTH args/result using `config.log_tool_calls_result_max_chars` (default 2000 via `br-1m6a.16`).

QUERY STATS
- Keep the current per-tool-call tracker approach in `lib.rs`:
  - Create a fresh `QueryTracker`, `enable()` it, set it as the active tracker for the call.
  - At end, snapshot for totals/time.
- Detailed per-table breakdown panel is handled by `br-1m6a.6`.

IMPLEMENTATION DIRECTION
- Prefer rendering panels with real `ftui` widgets into an offscreen `Frame` and exporting to ANSI (via `ftui-extras` `export::TextExporter::ansi()` once added as a dependency).
  - This removes fragile width/ANSI math.
- Keep the hot path cheap:
  - If gated off, avoid JSON cloning/pretty-printing.

TESTING
- Unit tests for gating:
  - `LOG_RICH_ENABLED=false` suppresses panels.
  - `TOOLS_LOG_ENABLED=false` suppresses panels.
  - `LOG_TOOL_CALLS_ENABLED=false` suppresses panels.
- Unit tests for truncation using config limit.
- Unit tests verifying masking helper is applied to args/result JSON.
- Snapshot-ish tests for panel structure (headers present) with ANSI-stripping normalization.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:00.153922469Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T23:39:30.253949322Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T23:39:30.252273375Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.2","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:00.153922469Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.2","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.18","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:25:55.144745410Z","created_by":"CobaltTower","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1m6a.20","title":"Alt-screen left split console: HUD + LogViewer log pane","description":"Implement the "left 30%" style console layout: a two-pane, AltScreen dashboard where the HUD is static on the left and a scrollable/searchable log viewer lives on the right.

WHY
- Inline mode preserves terminal scrollback, but it cannot do a true left/right HUD vs log split.
- Users explicitly want layouts like "left 30%" and want to configure this interactively.

CONFIG / INPUTS
- Uses layout config from `br-1m6a.19`:
  - `CONSOLE_SPLIT_MODE=left`
  - `CONSOLE_SPLIT_RATIO_PERCENT` (e.g., 30)

GATING
- Only engage split mode when ALL are true:
  - `config.log_rich_enabled == true`
  - `stdout.is_terminal() == true`
  - running in HTTP server mode (stdio mode cannot safely run an interactive UI loop)

CORE UI (ALTSCREEN)
- Use `ftui::ScreenMode::AltScreen` for the dashboard writer.
- Render a 2-column layout:
  1) Left pane: existing HUD (reuse `render_dashboard_frame` or a factored-out HUD widget)
  2) Right pane: a real log viewer widget (see below)

LOG VIEWER (USE FRANKENTUI WIDGET)
- Do NOT hand-roll a bespoke scroll/search UI.
- Use `ftui_widgets::log_viewer::{LogViewer, LogViewerState}`.
  - Demo reference: `/dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/log_search.rs`
- Behavior:
  - Follow mode on by default (tail -f semantics)
  - `/` opens search bar
  - `n` / `N`: next/prev match
  - `f`: filter toggle (show only matching lines)
  - Up/Down/PageUp/PageDown: scroll
  - End: jump to latest
  - `?`: help overlay

LOG ROUTING (CRITICAL)
- In AltScreen mode, `TerminalWriter::write_log()` is a no-op.
- Therefore ALL "console log lines" must be routed into an in-memory buffer that the log viewer renders.
- Proposed approach:
  - Add a `DashboardLogSink` abstraction owned by `StartupDashboard`:
    - Inline mode: sink writes to `TerminalWriter::write_log()`.
    - AltScreen mode: sink appends to a ring buffer feeding `LogViewerState`.
  - Update all call sites that currently call `dashboard_write_log()` so they work in both modes.

INTERACTION
- Read key input from `/dev/tty` (preferred) or stdin.
- Only enable interactive input in HTTP mode + TTY.
- Keybindings for layout should interoperate with `br-1m6a.19` (ratio adjust, toggle help, etc.).

PERF
- Keep last N lines (e.g., 5_000) to bound memory.
- Avoid expensive ANSI parsing on every render; store both raw and ANSI-stripped if needed.

TESTING (UNIT)
- Ratio -> column width calculation + clamp.
- Log ring buffer push/overflow.
- LogViewer state transitions for:
  - append + follow mode
  - search open/close
  - filter toggle
  - scroll bounds

E2E (HOOK INTO `br-1m6a.15`)
- Start server with `CONSOLE_SPLIT_MODE=left` + ratio.
- Assert the startup output includes the plain line `Console: left 30% (alt-screen)` (or similar) so tests don’t need to parse alt-screen control codes.

NOTES
- Default remains inline HUD + scrollback. Left split is opt-in.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:50:26.959533778Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T00:15:04.744630382Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T00:15:04.744534964Z","close_reason":"Implemented alt-screen split layout: LogPane wrapper in console.rs, split_columns calculator, render_split_frame two-pane renderer. Wired into lib.rs: StartupDashboard.log_pane, AltScreen mode in compute_writer_settings, log_line routing to ring buffer, render_now split path, handle_log_pane_key with scroll/search/follow bindings. Fixed 6 clippy warnings from br-1m6a.7 code. Committed dbf52f1.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.20","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:50:26.959533778Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.20","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:50:40.815547045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1m6a.21","title":"Command palette for console actions + layout tuning","description":"Add a FrankenTUI command palette to the rich console so users can discover and execute console actions (layout tuning, theme switching, toggles, copy links) without memorizing keybindings.

WHY
- We are adding many interactive console behaviors (layout config, log search, follow mode, filters). A command palette makes this discoverable and slick.
- FrankenTUI already ships a high-quality `CommandPalette` widget with deterministic matching modes and evidence tracking.
  - Demo reference: `/dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/command_palette_lab.rs`

SCOPE
- Implement the palette initially for AltScreen mode (`br-1m6a.20`) where we already have an event loop.
- Optional stretch: expose a minimal palette overlay in Inline mode too (only if safe).

UX
- Open palette with `Ctrl+P` (and `:` as an alternate).
- Palette shows:
  - action title
  - short hint / keybinding (if any)
  - optional category (Layout / Theme / Logs / Tools / Help)
- Palette supports:
  - prefix/substring/fuzzy matching
  - deterministic ordering (stable tests)

ACTIONS (MINIMUM)
Layout:
- Switch console mode: `inline`, `left split`
- Set split ratio: 20/30/40/50
- Increase/decrease HUD height percent (inline mode)
- Toggle anchor top/bottom
- Toggle inline auto-size
- Persist console settings now (writes `CONSOLE_*` keys to the user envfile from `br-1m6a.19`)

Theme (from `br-1m6a.12`):
- Cycle theme
- Set theme: `cyberpunk_aurora|darcula|lumen_light|nordic_frost|high_contrast`

Logs:
- Toggle follow mode (log viewer)
- Open search (`/`)
- Toggle filter-only-matches
- Clear log buffer (optional)

Tool panels (runtime toggles):
- Toggle `LOG_TOOL_CALLS_ENABLED`
- Toggle `TOOLS_LOG_ENABLED`

Help:
- Show keybindings overlay
- Show current config summary (sanitized)

PERSISTENCE / SAFETY
- Persistence is enabled by default when `CONSOLE_AUTO_SAVE=true` (from `br-1m6a.19`).
- Writes MUST go to the user envfile:
  - default: `~/.config/mcp-agent-mail/config.env`
  - override: `CONSOLE_PERSIST_PATH=/path/to/file`
- MUST NOT write to repo `.env`.
- Persist ONLY `CONSOLE_*` keys (layout + `CONSOLE_THEME`). Do not silently persist unrelated server config.

IMPLEMENTATION
- Add `ConsoleCommandPalette` state struct owned by the console runtime in AltScreen mode.
- Use `ftui_widgets::command_palette::{CommandPalette, ActionItem}`.
- On action selection:
  - update in-memory console state (apply live if possible)
  - if `CONSOLE_AUTO_SAVE=true`, persist via `mcp_agent_mail_core::config::update_envfile()`
  - show a confirmation toast (`br-1m6a.4`) or a one-line log fallback

TESTING
- Unit test action dispatch mapping (selecting action updates expected config fields).
- Unit test stable ordering of action list.
- Unit test persistence writes only `CONSOLE_*` keys.
- Render smoke test (no panic) for palette open/closed.

NOTES
- This bead is about console UX only; it must not change server semantics.","notes":"Implementation refinement (ftui demo: crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/command_palette_lab.rs)
- Use `ftui_widgets::command_palette::{CommandPalette, ActionItem, MatchFilter}` directly; avoid custom fuzzy logic.
- Enable evidence tracking in dev/test builds (`palette.enable_evidence_tracking(true)`) so we can debug ranking deterministically (and assert on evidence if needed).
- Match modes: consider cycling `MatchFilter::{All,Exact,Prefix,WordStart,Substring,Fuzzy}` via a key (like `Ctrl+M`) in AltScreen for easy tuning; keep default `Fuzzy`.
- Action IDs should be stable strings (e.g. `layout.inline`, `layout.split_left`, `logs.follow_toggle`, `tools.log_tool_calls_toggle`) so persistence/tests can target them.
- Determinism: action list ordering should be stable before matching (sort by category then title, tie-break by id) so tests do not depend on hash iteration.
- Optional but slick: expose a small "evidence ledger" side panel for the currently highlighted action (toggle with `Ctrl+E`) mirroring the demo; helpful when tuning the palette.

User-facing UX requirements
- Palette is the discoverability surface for interactive console configuration:
  - layout ratio and HUD height must be adjustable from the palette (not only hidden keybindings)
  - include "Reset layout to defaults" and "Reset theme to default" actions
- Auto-persist by default:
  - if `CONSOLE_AUTO_SAVE=true` (default), any layout/theme-changing action should persist immediately
  - keep an explicit "Persist now" action for `CONSOLE_AUTO_SAVE=false`
- Keep a small confirmation toast/log line after any action that changes layout/theme:
  - include the new value (e.g. "Split ratio: 30% (saved)")

E2E hook ideas (optional but high value)
- Under `AM_E2E_INTERACTIVE=1`, drive a real PTY:
  - open palette (`Ctrl+P`), type a query (e.g. `split 30`), hit Enter
  - assert `CONSOLE_SPLIT_RATIO_PERCENT=30` written to `CONSOLE_PERSIST_PATH`
  - assert a confirmation toast/line is present (after ANSI/OSC normalization)

Test ideas
- Pure unit tests for action dispatch: selecting action id mutates the expected in-memory state + persisted envfile (only `CONSOLE_*` keys) when enabled.
- Snapshot-ish tests on stripped output: opening palette renders a stable title + at least N action rows (no exact spacing).
- If evidence ledger implemented: assert evidence entries are non-empty for a fuzzy query like `log` and ordering is stable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:53:23.492545480Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T00:15:56.115687671Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T00:15:56.115647546Z","close_reason":"Implemented command palette: ConsoleCommandPalette in console.rs with 25 actions across 5 categories (Layout, Theme, Logs, Tools, Help). Wired into lib.rs: Ctrl+P/:  opens palette, full event routing, dispatch_palette_action for layout changes, theme switching via ftui_extras, log pane controls, runtime tool logging toggles. Extracted persist_console_settings(). 9 unit tests. Committed d601d55.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.21","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:53:23.492545480Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.21","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:53:30.598012784Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.21","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:53:30.495288156Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-1m6a.22","title":"Structured event timeline view (AltScreen)","description":"Add an AltScreen-only structured event timeline view (filters + details) so Agent Mail feels like an observability cockpit, not just a wall of logs.

WHY
- Logs are useful, but a structured timeline makes it instantly clear what happened: tool calls, messages, conflicts, ack escalations, etc.
- FrankenTUI has a strong reference implementation for an event stream viewer.
  - Demo reference: `/dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/action_timeline.rs`

SCOPE
- AltScreen mode only (build on `br-1m6a.20`).
- Timeline is a *view* over the same internal events we already emit as log lines.

DATA MODEL
- Introduce a small internal `ConsoleEvent` struct (server crate) with:
  - `ts` (monotonic sequence or ISO timestamp)
  - `kind` (tool_call_start/tool_call_end/http_request/message_sent/reservation_conflict/etc)
  - `severity` (info/warn/error)
  - `summary` (short)
  - `fields: Vec<(String,String)>` (sanitized)
  - optional `json: serde_json::Value` (sanitized)

BUFFER
- Bounded ring buffer (e.g., last 500 events) stored in `StartupDashboard` so it is available to UI render.

UI
- Split right pane into:
  - Timeline list with filters (severity/kind/project/agent)
  - Detail panel showing sanitized fields + optional pretty JSON (syntax highlighted)
- Navigation:
  - Up/Down select
  - Enter toggles detail focus
  - `f` cycles filters
  - `/` search summaries
  - `?` help overlay

INTEGRATION
- Emit `ConsoleEvent` alongside existing log lines in these code paths:
  - tools/call start/end
  - request panel emission
  - send/reply/handshake tools
  - file reservation conflict detection
- Always sanitize via `br-1m6a.18` before storing.

COMMAND PALETTE
- Add a palette action (from `br-1m6a.21`) to toggle the right pane between:
  - Log Viewer
  - Event Timeline

TESTING
- Unit tests for ring buffer overflow + stable ordering.
- Unit tests for filter logic.
- Render smoke test (no panic) for empty timeline.

NOTE
- This is a polish/showcase feature (P2). It should not block core parity work.","notes":"Implementation refinement (ftui demo: crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/action_timeline.rs)
- Model the timeline as a bounded `VecDeque<ConsoleEvent>` (MAX_EVENTS ~500) plus UI state: `selected`, `scroll_offset`, `follow`, `show_details`, and filter toggles.
- Filtering should be applied as a view over the ring buffer (do not duplicate events); selection should clamp and follow mode should keep the latest selected.
- Keep event IDs monotonic (`next_id: u64`) for stable ordering and debug.
- Consider severity/kind filters cycling like the demo (single-key cycles through enums + None).
- Details panel: show `summary`, `fields`, and (if present) a pretty JSON block; syntax highlight with the active theme’s syntax theme.
- Event ingestion: emit structured events at the same points we already log; store sanitized fields only (masking + known-key sanitization).

UX integration notes
- Timeline view switching must be discoverable via the command palette (`br-1m6a.21`) and should not introduce keybinding conflicts with the LogViewer search UX.
- Keep all stored fields sanitized at ingestion time so the detail panel cannot leak secrets even if rendered verbatim.

Test ideas
- Ring buffer eviction: pushing MAX_EVENTS+N keeps newest, preserves ordering by id.
- Filter logic: each filter reduces the visible set correctly; search over summaries respects case-insensitivity.
- Follow mode: when enabled, selection moves to newest event after append; when disabled, selection stays put.
- Render smoke tests: empty timeline, one-event timeline, and narrow widths should not panic.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:53:48.030861146Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T00:11:39.723819726Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.22","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:53:48.030861146Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.22","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.18","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:53:53.751535168Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.22","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:53:53.543676485Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.22","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.21","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:53:53.646735640Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- Details panel: show `summary`, `fields`, and (if present) a pretty JSON block; syntax highlight with the active theme’s syntax theme.

UX integration notes
- Timeline view switching must be discoverable via the command palette (`br-1m6a.21`) and should not introduce keybinding conflicts with the LogViewer search UX.
- Keep all stored fields sanitized at ingestion time so the detail panel cannot leak secrets even if rendered verbatim.

Test ideas
- Ring buffer eviction: pushing MAX_EVENTS+N keeps newest, preserves ordering by id.
- Filter logic: each filter reduces the visible set correctly; search over summaries respects case-insensitivity.
- Follow mode: when enabled, selection moves to newest event after append; when disabled, selection stays put.
- Render smoke tests: empty timeline, one-event timeline, and narrow widths should not panic.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltTower","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:53:48.030861146Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T00:55:38.506732612Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.22","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:53:48.030861146Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.22","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.18","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:53:53.751535168Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.22","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:53:53.543676485Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.22","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.21","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:53:53.646735640Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1m6a.23","title":"Console capabilities + discoverability (banner + help)","description":"Add a clear, FrankenTUI-native "Console Capabilities" + discoverability block so users instantly understand what terminal features are active (and why something may not be working).

WHY
- We lean hard on terminal features (AltScreen split, OSC-8 hyperlinks, mouse scroll, palette, log search). When something doesn't work, users need a single place to confirm whether it is a terminal limitation vs config.
- FrankenTUI already has strong capability detection and an evidence-ledger model.
  - Demo reference: `/dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/terminal_capabilities.rs`

SCOPE
- Rich console only (TTY + `LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true`).
- Add:
  1) A small startup banner section: "Console Capabilities".
  2) The `?` help overlay (inline + AltScreen) should include the same capabilities plus the key discovery hints (`Ctrl+P` palette, `/` search in LogViewer, `?` help).
  3) A grep-friendly, stable-format one-liner for tests/debugging:
     - `ConsoleCaps: tc=1 osc8=1 mouse=0 sync=1 kitty=0 focus=0`
     - Exact numeric values may vary by terminal/CI; the *keys* and format must be stable.
- Must be enabled by default; no user action required.

IMPLEMENTATION
- Introduce a small `ConsoleCaps` struct in the server crate, computed once at startup from FrankenTUI/terminal capability detection.
- Use the caps to gate behavior where appropriate:
  - OSC-8 hyperlinks: only emit when `osc8=1` (still always include raw URL text).
  - Mouse-only UX (hover tooltips, scroll targeting): only enable when `mouse=1`.
- Degrade gracefully:
  - Non-TTY or `LOG_RICH_ENABLED=false`: do not emit capability panels; keep output plain/grep-friendly.

TESTING
- Unit tests:
  - formatting of `ConsoleCaps:` line is ASCII-only and stable.
  - caps-to-string never panics on "unknown" values.
- Render/integration tests:
  - banner contains "Console Capabilities" header after ANSI/OSC normalization.
- E2E (`br-1m6a.15`):
  - in a PTY run, assert `ConsoleCaps:` line exists (do not assert exact values; assert presence of all keys).

NOTES
- If FrankenTUI exposes an evidence ledger for caps, optionally show a compact "why" hint in the help overlay (not required for MVP).","notes":"E2E constraint (PTY capture)
- The `ConsoleCaps:` one-liner needs to be emitted via a guaranteed plain-text path (stderr log line) so `script` PTY transcripts capture it even when the console switches into AltScreen.
- Same for the split/layout summary line used by br-1m6a.15: emit it *before* entering AltScreen.

Implementation hint
- Prefer a single early emission like:
  - `Console: split=left ratio=30% (alt-screen)`
  - `ConsoleCaps: tc=1 osc8=1 mouse=0 sync=1 kitty=0 focus=0`
  emitted right after capability detection and before creating the AltScreen writer.

Test hook
- br-1m6a.15 should assert presence of all ConsoleCaps keys (not values) in the normalized transcript.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-07T00:12:08.014743363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T00:42:21.137531707Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.23","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T00:12:08.014743363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1m6a.3","title":"Sparkline throughput trends in live HUD","description":"Improve the live HUD sparkline(s) to show real, intuitive throughput trends (and showcase FrankenTUI sparkline rendering).

CURRENT STATE
- A request sparkline already exists:
  - `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/console.rs::SparklineBuffer`
  - Sampled every ~1200ms by `StartupDashboard::spawn_refresh_worker()`
  - Rendered in `render_dashboard_frame()` using `ftui::widgets::sparkline::Sparkline::render_to_string()`.
- The HUD currently labels the sparkline as `req/s` even though it is actually `req/tick` (approx).

TARGET STATE
1) Correct units + normalization
- Normalize request counts to true `req/s` using the actual tick interval.
  - Keep a constant `DASHBOARD_TICK_MS` (currently 1200) and compute `req_per_sec = count * 1000 / tick_ms`.
- Display BOTH:
  - Numeric value (e.g. `req/s: 12.5`)
  - Sparkline visualization

2) Ring buffer correctness
- Keep exactly `SPARKLINE_CAPACITY` points without O(n) shifting.
  - Prefer `VecDeque` or a simple circular buffer.

3) Optional additional sparklines (stretch)
- Latency trend sparkline (avg latency per tick).
- Message throughput sparkline (send/reply counts per tick).

4) Theme integration
- Sparkline gradient should derive from the semantic theme (`br-1m6a.12`).

TESTING
- Unit test normalization math (req/tick -> req/s).
- Unit test ring buffer bounds/overflow.
- Unit test render does not panic for empty/all-zero data.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:00.254139919Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T23:39:30.255300531Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T23:39:30.252273375Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.3","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:00.254139919Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1m6a.4","title":"Toast notifications for important agent events","description":"Add toast notifications for important agent events, using FrankenTUI's real notification queue widgets.

WHY
- Agent Mail is event-heavy (registering agents, conflicts, releases, ack escalations). A HUD without toasts forces users to scan scrollback.
- FrankenTUI already ships a polished notification stack with priority ordering, dedup, actions, and positioning.
  - Demo reference: `/dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/notifications.rs`

GOALS
- Toasts are enabled by default when rich console is active in a real TTY.
- Non-HUD / non-TTY contexts must stay grep-friendly: degrade to a plain log line (no ANSI art dumps).
- Keep behavior testable without sleeps.

IMPLEMENTATION
1) Use FrankenTUI queue types
- Add to `StartupDashboard`:
  - `notifications: Mutex<ftui_widgets::notification_queue::NotificationQueue>`
- Queue config (initial):
  - `max_visible`: 3-4
  - `max_queued`: 20
  - position: `ToastPosition::TopRight` (or inside HUD area if we restrict drawing to the HUD rect)

2) API surface
- `fn push_toast(&self, toast: Toast, prio: NotificationPriority)`
- Convenience helpers:
  - `toast_info(msg)`, `toast_warning(msg)`, `toast_error(msg)`

3) Rendering
- In the dashboard render path, render `NotificationStack::new(&queue)` as an overlay.
  - If overlaying over the whole terminal is not safe in Inline mode, render it inside the HUD area only.

4) Non-HUD degradation
- If no dashboard is active (stdio mode or non-TTY), toast events become plain one-line logs.
  - Prefer `console_info/console_warning/console_error` once `br-1m6a.5` exists.
  - Otherwise: `tracing::info!/warn!/error!` or `ftui_runtime::ftui_println!()` with plain text.

EVENT HOOKS (INITIAL SET)
- In tool dispatch/instrumentation paths, trigger toasts for:
  - `register_agent` success: "Agent {name} registered ({program})"
  - `file_reservation_paths`: conflict -> warning toast with conflict summary
  - `force_release_file_reservation` success -> warning toast
  - repeated server errors: if >5 errors in 10s -> error toast
  - messaging bursts: if >20 send/reply in 5s -> info toast

ACTIONS (OPTIONAL, NICE-TO-HAVE)
- For some toasts, include actions:
  - "Copy endpoint" (copies URL to clipboard if available; otherwise just logs)
  - "Open web UI" (only if we ever support shell-out; otherwise omit)

TESTING
- Unit tests for queue behavior (no sleeps):
  - push respects max_queued
  - urgent priority jumps ahead
  - dismiss/dismiss_all behavior
  - auto-expiry can be simulated by mutating `toast.state.created_at` to an old instant, then calling `queue.tick(...)` (no wall-clock sleeps)
- Render smoke test: overlay render does not panic for empty/1/4 visible toasts.

NOTE
- If time-based animations make tests flaky, disable animations for our toasts (use reduced motion config) and keep assertions on structure/content only.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:00.360557085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T23:39:30.256372808Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T23:39:30.252273375Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.4","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:00.360557085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
